title: neomammillaria hemisphaerica
intro: Neomammillaria hemisphaerica, commonly known as the hemisphere cactus or button cactus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cactaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and is prized for its small, globular stems that can grow up to 4 inches in diameter. Neomammillaria hemisphaerica has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which are believed to provide health benefits such as wound healing, skin protection, and immune system support.
